headlines.title: UN chief fears for ‘millions’ of Palestinians amid UNRWA funding shortfall
headlines.snippet: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres expressed concern for millions of Palestinians due to a funding shortfall for the UN agency for Palestinian refugees (UNRWA). He denounced what he called disinformation and smear campaigns against the agency.

headlines.title: Iran says it couldn’t export a ‘single barrel of oil’ during US blockade
headlines.snippet: Iran stated it was unable to export any oil during a US blockade of its ports. This was according to the country's chief negotiator, who highlighted the inability to ship crude during that period.

headlines.title: US lifts restrictions on powerful AI models Fable, Mythos, Anthropic says
headlines.snippet: AI firm Anthropic announced it will restore access to its powerful AI models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5. This follows the removal of US export controls that had previously restricted access to these technologies.

headlines.title: Southeast Asia’s homegrown artists are knocking K-pop off its pedestal
headlines.snippet: Southeast Asia's music scene is seeing its homegrown artists gain prominence, challenging the dominance of K-pop. These artists are drawing inspiration from K-pop's success to develop their own unique regional sound and appeal.

headlines.title: Algeria to vote in test of post-Hirak political landscape
headlines.snippet: Algeria is holding legislative elections, which will serve as a test of the country's post-Hirak political landscape. The elections are taking place amid ongoing debates concerning reform, voter turnout, and overall political stability.

headlines.title: Iran says it is pursuing diplomacy but remains ready for war
headlines.snippet: Iran's chief negotiator stated that Tehran prioritizes diplomacy with the US but remains prepared for war. He indicated that if diplomatic dialogue is not implemented, Iran will respond accordingly, emphasizing readiness for conflict.

headlines.title: Qatari prime minister meets US envoys for talks on Iran negotiations
headlines.snippet: The Qatari prime minister met with US envoys in Doha to discuss ongoing negotiations between Iran and the United States. The meeting reaffirmed Qatar's mediation efforts and support for the talks stemming from a memorandum of understanding.

headlines.title: IRGC says it killed six separatists in northwestern Iran
headlines.snippet: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) reported killing six separatists in northwestern Iran. The IRGC stated it foiled a cell attempting to enter the country to carry out acts of sabotage and terrorism.

headlines.title: Iran exports 50 million barrels of crude after US lifts blockade
headlines.snippet: Iran has exported 50 million barrels of crude oil since the United States lifted its naval blockade on the country's energy exports. This marks a significant resumption of oil sales following the lifting of sanctions.

headlines.title: Vance says oil traffic through Strait of Hormuz has returned to pre-war levels
headlines.snippet: US Vice President JD Vance stated that oil traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has returned to pre-war levels. He noted that some days see more oil exiting the Strait than even before the war began.

headlines.title: US NATO Envoy Presses Europe to Bear More Ukraine Cost
headlines.snippet: The US NATO Envoy is pressing European allies to assume a greater share of the costs associated with supporting Ukraine. This call highlights the ongoing need for international burden-sharing in addressing the conflict.

headlines.title: Vance Says ‘More Game to Play’ in Iran Negotiations
headlines.snippet: JD Vance indicated that there are further developments expected in the negotiations involving Iran. This suggests that the diplomatic process is ongoing and may yield additional outcomes.

headlines.title: Venezuela quake deaths rise to 1,943, lawmaker says
headlines.snippet: The death toll from earthquakes in Venezuela has risen to 1,943, according to a lawmaker. This figure reflects the significant human impact of the recent seismic activity in the country.

headlines.title: Can Lebanon Really Disarm Hezbollah?
headlines.snippet: The question of whether Lebanon can disarm Hezbollah is a complex issue being discussed. It involves significant political and security considerations within the region.

headlines.title: Israeli fighter jets scrambled after airliner accidentally reports hijacking
headlines.snippet: Israeli fighter jets were scrambled after an airliner mistakenly reported a hijacking. The incident caused a temporary security alert for the aircraft and air traffic control.

headlines.title: Syrian parliament set to announce Sharaa's pick of 70 MPs
headlines.snippet: Syria's parliament is set to announce the selection of 70 Members of Parliament (MPs). Following this announcement, the new legislative body will commence its work of debating and approving new laws.

headlines.title: Man arrested after arson attack on Dublin Islamic centre
headlines.snippet: A man has been arrested following an arson attack on an Islamic center in Dublin. The incident occurred after worshippers had finished their afternoon prayers at Dublin's Al Madinah Hall.

headlines.title: US Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to end birthright citizenship
headlines.snippet: The US Supreme Court rejected a bid by Donald Trump to end birthright citizenship. Chief Justice John Roberts affirmed that children born in the United States are citizens at birth under the Fourteenth Amendment.

headlines.title: Iran prepares for late supreme leader's funeral later this week
headlines.snippet: Iran is preparing for the funeral of its late supreme leader, which is scheduled to take place later this week. Representatives from approximately 30 countries are expected to attend the ceremony.

headlines.title: Fresh Israeli strikes on Gaza set 200 tents ablaze despite truce
headlines.snippet: Fresh Israeli strikes in Gaza have set approximately 200 tents ablaze, despite an existing ceasefire. These strikes have left thousands of people without shelter.

headlines.title: حملة من اليمين المتطرف الإسرائيلي تستهدف مدارس الفلسطينيين
headlines.snippet: An extremist Israeli campaign is targeting Palestinian schools. This initiative raises concerns about educational access and potential disruption for Palestinian students.

headlines.title: "نبيل فهمي"أمينا عاما لجامعة الدول العربية
headlines.snippet: Nabil Fahmy has been appointed as the Secretary-General of the Arab League. This appointment marks a significant leadership change within the regional organization.

headlines.title: النائبة الديمقراطية "الكساندرا كورتيز"تطرح تعديلاً لمنع نقل الأسلحة لـ"إسرائيل"
headlines.snippet: Democratic Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez has proposed an amendment to prevent the transfer of weapons to Israel. This legislative move signals a debate regarding US arms sales policy.

headlines.title: حرب السرديات... كيف تعيد إيران رسم معركة التأثير على الوعي العالمي؟
headlines.snippet: Iran is engaged in a narrative war, seeking to shape global perceptions. This involves efforts to influence international understanding of its actions and positions.

headlines.title: تداعيات التطورات الإقليمية على العلاقات الإيرانية – الأميركية
headlines.snippet: Regional developments are having implications for the relationship between Iran and the United States. These shifts are influencing diplomatic and strategic interactions between the two nations.

headlines.title: Araghchi: Iraq is preparing to hold magnificent funeral procession ceremony for Martyr Ayatollah Khamenei
headlines.snippet: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Iraq is preparing to hold a funeral ceremony for Grand Ayatollah Khamenei. He noted that Iraq, like Iran, is making arrangements for the event.

headlines.title: Pezeshkian: Iran, India have great potentials to expand cooperation
headlines.snippet: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian told Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi that Iran and India possess substantial potential for expanding cooperation. He highlighted opportunities for increased bilateral relations.

headlines.title: Araghchi to US Homeland Security Secretary: You proved that you are not worthy of hosting a tournament
headlines.snippet: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi responded to remarks by US Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin, stating that the US has proven unworthy of hosting a tournament. This comment suggests a dispute over international hosting rights.

headlines.title: Doha talks will center on MoU, no separate meeting with US on agenda, says Baqaei
headlines.snippet: Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei rejected claims of a planned meeting between Iranian and American delegations in Doha. He stated that discussions will focus on implementing the provisions of the Islamabad memorandum of understanding (MoU).

headlines.title: Government spokesperson praises navy’s role in safeguarding maritime security during recent war
headlines.snippet: Government spokesperson Fatemeh Mohajerani praised the navy's crucial role in safeguarding maritime security during the recent war. She highlighted the navy's performance in countering threats at sea.

headlines.title: No talks until US abides by MoU provisions: Ghalibaf
headlines.snippet: Ghalibaf stated that no talks will occur until the US abides by the provisions of the MoU. This condition sets a prerequisite for future negotiations between Iran and the United States.

headlines.title: Iran, India have great potentials to expand relations: Pres.
headlines.snippet: President stated that Iran and India have significant potential to expand their relations. This indicates a positive outlook for bilateral cooperation between the two countries.

headlines.title: Loud car explosion reported in Haifa
headlines.snippet: A loud car explosion was reported in Haifa. Details regarding the cause or any casualties have not been immediately provided.

headlines.title: Qatar says no Iran, US high-level talks to take place
headlines.snippet: Qatar has stated that no high-level talks between Iran and the US are scheduled to take place. This clarification addresses speculation about potential diplomatic meetings.

headlines.title: Gaza death toll reaches 73,066 since Oct. 07: health min.
headlines.snippet: The health ministry reported that the death toll in Gaza has reached 73,066 since October 7. This figure represents the cumulative casualties reported in the region.

headlines.title: Atlas of Iran’s Missile Cities - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: An 'Atlas of Iran’s Missile Cities' has been published. The publication details information regarding Iran's missile infrastructure.

headlines.title: Khamenei to end Eje'i’s judiciary tenure after one term - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: Khamenei is reportedly set to end Eje'i’s judiciary tenure after one term. This indicates a change in the leadership of Iran's judicial branch.

headlines.title: Deadly attacks shake northwest Iran as IRGC reports new clashes - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: Deadly attacks have shaken northwestern Iran, with the IRGC reporting new clashes. These incidents suggest ongoing security challenges in the region.

headlines.title: Two-week banking disruption leaves Iranians struggling to access money - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: A two-week banking disruption has left Iranians struggling to access their money. This financial issue is impacting the daily lives of citizens.

headlines.title: Nearly half of Iranians live in land subsidence zones, report warns - ایران اینترنشنال
headlines.snippet: A report warns that nearly half of Iranians live in land subsidence zones. This highlights a significant environmental and geological concern for the country.

headlines.title: In south Lebanon, Netanyahu says Israel will stay as long as Hizbollah 'threatens us'
headlines.snippet: In southern Lebanon, Netanyahu stated that Israel will remain as long as Hezbollah poses a threat. This declaration underscores the ongoing security concerns along the border.

headlines.title: Thousands expected as Vespa celebrates 80 years in Rome
headlines.snippet: Thousands of people are expected to gather in Rome as Vespa celebrates its 80th anniversary. This event marks a significant milestone for the iconic Italian brand.

headlines.title: RJ following up on crew bus collision in New York
headlines.snippet: Royal Jordanian (RJ) is following up on a collision involving a crew bus in New York. The incident is under investigation, and RJ is addressing the situation.

headlines.title: GDP rises to 2.9% in Q1 2026, up from 2.7% in 2025 — DoS
headlines.snippet: The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) rose to 2.9% in the first quarter of 2026, an increase from 2.7% in 2025, according to the Department of Statistics (DoS). This indicates economic growth for the period.

headlines.title: Jordan, Brazil seek to deepen trade ties ahead of São Paulo economic forum
headlines.snippet: Jordan and Brazil are seeking to deepen trade ties ahead of an economic forum in São Paulo. The aim is to enhance commercial relationships between the two nations.

headlines.title: Dangerous heat wave to grip US as nation marks 250 years
headlines.snippet: A dangerous heat wave is expected to grip the US as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary. This extreme weather event poses significant health risks.

headlines.title: Financial gap: BDL revises government plan, leaving depositors to wait longer
headlines.snippet: A financial gap has emerged, leading the BDL to revise a government plan. Depositors will reportedly have to wait longer for their funds.

headlines.title: 'Campaigns targeting the army and its leadership will not affect its action,' Aoun says
headlines.snippet: Aoun stated that campaigns targeting the army and its leadership will not affect its actions. This assertion emphasizes the army's resolve amidst criticism.

headlines.title: 'Security' annex to Lebanon-Israel agreement revealed: 'Hezbollah disarmament' for 'gradual' Israeli army withdrawal
headlines.snippet: A 'security' annex to the Lebanon-Israel agreement has been revealed. It reportedly includes Hezbollah disarmament in exchange for a gradual Israeli army withdrawal.

headlines.title: UAE allows citizens to travel to Lebanon again
headlines.snippet: The UAE has permitted its citizens to travel to Lebanon again. This decision eases travel restrictions between the two countries.

headlines.title: More than half of social media child safety features aren’t working as advertised, new research finds
headlines.snippet: New research indicates that over half of social media child safety features do not function as advertised. These tools are intended to protect young users online.

headlines.title: Witkoff and Kushner will not meet with Iranians in Doha, Qatar says
headlines.snippet: Qatar's Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated that US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will meet Qatari mediators, but not Iranian officials. This clarifies the diplomatic arrangements in Doha.

headlines.title: Little progress achieved in two weeks since Iran and US signed agreement
headlines.snippet: Little progress has been achieved in the two weeks since Iran and the US signed an agreement. The agreement was intended to offer a path towards ending the war in the region.

headlines.title: Dangerous and likely record-setting heat wave to scorch millions July Fourth week
headlines.snippet: A dangerous and potentially record-setting heat wave is predicted to affect millions of Americans during the July Fourth week. This extreme weather event coincides with national celebrations.

headlines.title: Egypt’s tourism sector continues its upward trajectory, seeking new phase beyond beaches and resorts
headlines.snippet: Egypt's tourism sector is showing continued growth, aiming for new phases beyond traditional beach and resort offerings. Reports praise the sector's strong performance and increasing visitor numbers.

headlines.title: بوركينا فاسو بعد مالي والنيجر: نُخب أفريقيا الجديدة تلفظ النفوذ الفرنسي - Al Akhbar
headlines.snippet: Burkina Faso, following Mali and Niger, is seeing its new elites reject French influence. This trend signifies a shift in post-colonial relationships in the region.

headlines.title: قاليباف: قواتنا المسلحة ضمان تنفيذ التفاهم وليس قرارات مجلس الأمن - Al Akhbar
headlines.snippet: Qalibaf stated that Iran's armed forces are a guarantee for the implementation of understanding, not Security Council decisions. This emphasizes the role of military power in enforcing agreements.

headlines.title: وزير الخارجية العراقي: نرفض استهداف الخليج والحرب على إيران - Al Akhbar
headlines.snippet: The Iraqi Foreign Minister stated that Iraq rejects targeting the Gulf and war against Iran. This position signals Iraq's stance on regional security and conflict.

headlines.title: ضغوط كبيرة على النظام الصحي في فنزويلا بعد الزلزالين - Al Akhbar
headlines.snippet: Significant pressure is being placed on Venezuela's health system following two earthquakes. The seismic events have created substantial challenges for healthcare services.

headlines.snippet: The Supreme Court rejected Trump's request to restrict birthright citizenship for those born in the United States. The ruling upholds the principle of birthright citizenship.

headlines.title: الخارجية الروسية: الناتو يسعى إلى تعزيز وجوده في منطقة البحر الأسود التي تعد ذات أهمية استراتيجية بالنسبة لروسيا
headlines.snippet: The Russian Foreign Ministry commented that NATO seeks to increase its presence in the Black Sea region, which is of strategic importance to Russia. This highlights geopolitical tensions.

headlines.snippet: The Irish Islamic Council stated they will not be intimidated by those seeking to divide communities through fear and hatred. This is in response to an act of vandalism.

headlines.snippet: The Wall Street Journal, citing US officials, reported that Trump has decided to focus on diplomatic talks for the time being. This indicates a current preference for negotiation.

headlines.snippet: The Irish Islamic Council strongly condemned the shocking arson attack on a mosque in Dublin. They described the act as an attempt to incite division and hatred.

headlines.snippet: The Russian National Security Council expressed hope that the United States and Iran will reach a legally binding agreement. This reflects Russia's interest in a stable resolution.

headlines.title: UN chief: Supporting UNRWA is international community's responsibility toward Palestine refugees - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: The UN chief stated that supporting UNRWA is the international community's responsibility towards Palestinian refugees. This emphasizes the crucial role of the agency.

headlines.title: Tubas Governorate, Colonization and Wall Resistance Commission obtain decision to freeze demolition of residential structures in northern Jordan Valley - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: The Tubas Governorate and the Colonization and Wall Resistance Commission have obtained a decision to freeze the demolition of residential structures in the northern Jordan Valley. This action aims to protect Palestinian homes.

headlines.title: Two citizens killed, others injured in Israeli drone strike north of Khan Yunis - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: Two citizens were killed and others injured in an Israeli drone strike north of Khan Yunis. This incident underscores the ongoing conflict in the region.

headlines.title: Cabinet approves national plan to enhance resilience of Palestinian electricity system - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: The Cabinet has approved a national plan to enhance the resilience of the Palestinian electricity system. This initiative aims to improve critical infrastructure.

headlines.title: Vice President Al Sheikh discusses Yatta situation and escalating colonists’ attacks with Fatah delegation - WAFA Agency
headlines.snippet: Vice President Al Sheikh discussed the situation in Yatta and escalating colonists' attacks with a Fatah delegation. The meeting focused on addressing security concerns.

headlines.title: بإشراف منظمة الصحة العالمية.. إجلاء 30 مريضًا من غزة عبر رفح
headlines.snippet: Under the supervision of the World Health Organization, 30 patients were evacuated from Gaza via Rafah. This medical evacuation aims to provide critical care.

headlines.title: ألف يوم من الإبادة الإسرائيلية وهدنة تحت النار
headlines.snippet: A thousand days of Israeli aggression and a truce under fire have been noted. This highlights the prolonged nature of the conflict and its impact.

headlines.snippet: A Hamas delegation arrived in Cairo to discuss specific issues. The purpose of their visit is to engage in important discussions.

headlines.title: نادي الأسير يدعو الصليب الأحمر إلى التحرك العاجل لاستئناف زيارة الأسرى
headlines.snippet: The Prisoners' Club called on the Red Cross to take urgent action to resume visits to prisoners. This appeal seeks to ensure the rights and welfare of detainees.

headlines.snippet: Gaza's health ministry reported 8 deaths and 26 injuries in the past 24 hours. This update reflects the ongoing casualties in the region.

headlines.title: 9 children killed as tricycle plunges into a canal in Egypt
headlines.snippet: Nine children were killed when a tricycle plunged into a canal in Egypt. This tragic accident highlights a devastating loss of life.

headlines.title: 3 UN peacekeepers wounded in attack on C.Africa base
headlines.snippet: Three UN peacekeepers were wounded in an attack on a base in the Central African Republic. This incident underscores the dangers faced by peacekeepers.

headlines.title: Three-year-old rescued six days after Venezuela quakes
headlines.snippet: A three-year-old child was rescued six days after the earthquakes in Venezuela. This successful rescue offers a glimmer of hope amidst devastation.

headlines.title: Saudi ambassador receives industry minister in Beirut
headlines.snippet: The Saudi ambassador received the industry minister in Beirut. This meeting signifies diplomatic engagement between the two countries.

headlines.title: Pakistan interior minister arrives in Riyadh
headlines.snippet: Pakistan's interior minister arrived in Riyadh. This visit indicates ongoing cooperation and discussions between Pakistan and Saudi Arabia.

headlines.title: Trump criticises Supreme Court birthright citizenship ruling, urges Congress to act
headlines.snippet: Trump criticized the Supreme Court's ruling on birthright citizenship and urged Congress to take action. He disagrees with the court's decision on this matter.

headlines.title: Saudi Arabia extends work permit adjustment deadline until year-end
headlines.snippet: Saudi Arabia has extended the deadline for work permit adjustments until the end of the year. This provides additional time for individuals to comply with regulations.

headlines.title: Royal Jordanian crew bus involved in New York road accident
headlines.snippet: A Royal Jordanian crew bus was involved in a road accident in New York. The incident is being investigated, and the airline is addressing the situation.

headlines.title: Imtiaz expands its Dubai Islands portfolio with groundbreaking of Dh600m Sea Cliff Residence
headlines.snippet: Imtiaz is expanding its Dubai Islands portfolio with the groundbreaking of the Dh600 million Sea Cliff Residence. This marks a new development project.

headlines.title: UAE petrol, diesel prices for July 2026 announced
headlines.snippet: UAE petrol and diesel prices for July 2026 have been announced. This provides information on fuel costs for the upcoming month.

headlines.title: Former Israeli prime minister says Netanyahu 'incapable of governing' as far-right ministers 'control' him
headlines.snippet: A former Israeli prime minister stated that Netanyahu is 'incapable of governing' as far-right ministers 'control' him. He believes prolonged wars are harming Israel.

headlines.title: JD Vance says Iran's denial of Doha talks 'Persian negotiating tactic' as US envoys traveled to Qatar
headlines.snippet: JD Vance described Iran's denial of Doha talks as a 'Persian negotiating tactic' as US envoys traveled to Qatar. He suggested that technical talks are underway despite public rejections.

headlines.title: Israel to postpone withdrawal from 2 pilot areas in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Israel has decided to postpone its withdrawal from two pilot areas in southern Lebanon. The withdrawal from Zawtar and Frun will be delayed, according to Israeli sources.

headlines.title: Board of Peace to soon begin managing humanitarian shelter centers in Gaza: Report
headlines.snippet: The Board of Peace will soon manage humanitarian shelter centers in Gaza. The Tel al-Sultan area near Rafah is reportedly the first destination for civilians.

headlines.title: US House passes resolution to disclose sexual misconduct settlements paid by taxpayers
headlines.snippet: The US House passed a resolution to disclose sexual misconduct settlements paid by taxpayers. The Ethics Committee is directed to publish names and amounts within 60 days.

headlines.title: Erdoğan rejects Israeli accusations, says Türkiye 'will not heed slander'
headlines.snippet: Erdoğan rejected Israeli accusations, stating that Türkiye 'will not heed slander.' He asserted Türkiye's stance against what he termed false claims.

headlines.title: Türkiye prepared for all scenarios: President Erdoğan
headlines.snippet: President Erdoğan stated that Türkiye is prepared for all scenarios. This declaration indicates readiness to address potential future challenges.

headlines.title: NATO parliamentary delegation pays visit to Turkish drone giant Baykar
headlines.snippet: A NATO parliamentary delegation visited Baykar, a Turkish drone manufacturer. The visit highlights cooperation in defense technology.

headlines.title: Türkiye's strategic role for NATO's future amid Ankara summit
headlines.snippet: Türkiye's strategic role for NATO's future is being discussed, particularly in the context of the upcoming Ankara summit. This emphasizes Türkiye's importance within the alliance.

headlines.snippet: Rubio and Saddam Haftar discussed efforts to reunify Libya. This meeting indicates engagement on the political future of Libya.

headlines.snippet: Erdoğan criticized Israel's move concerning the events of 1915. This statement reflects Turkey's perspective on historical events.

headlines.snippet: Erdoğan received EU's Kallas in Ankara. This meeting signifies diplomatic engagement between Turkey and the European Union.

headlines.snippet: Özel criticized the security clampdown ahead of the NATO summit. This statement raises concerns about public order measures.

headlines.snippet: Türkiye is considering purchasing SAMP/T and Patriot missile systems, according to the Defense Minister. This indicates potential defense procurements.

headlines.snippet: A parcel bomb blast in Monaco wounded a Ukrainian oligarch. The incident is under investigation.

headlines.title: Trump announces first pre-midterm Republican national convention
headlines.snippet: Trump announced the first pre-midterm Republican national convention. This event is planned to galvanize support for the upcoming elections.

headlines.title: Live blog - US-Israeli war on Iran
headlines.snippet: A live blog is covering the US-Israeli war on Iran. This provides ongoing updates on the conflict.

headlines.title: War on Iran
headlines.snippet: There are no talks scheduled between Iran and the US. Qatar has stated that high-level meetings will not take place.

headlines.title: EU officials stress need for stronger ties with Türkiye after Ankara talks
headlines.snippet: EU officials emphasized the need for stronger ties with Türkiye following talks in Ankara. This highlights the importance of bilateral relations.

headlines.title: France's Greens challenge government with no confidence vote over heatwave response
headlines.snippet: France's Greens are challenging the government with a no-confidence vote over its response to the heatwave. This political move signals dissatisfaction with the government's actions.
